title: Fibromyalgia
chief_complaint: I hurt all over for the past 6 months. My muscles ache, I'm exhausted, I can't sleep, and I feel like I'm in a fog. I've been to 4 doctors and all my tests are normal. They say it's 'just stress'.

physical_exam.Tender_points: Widespread tenderness at 11/18 tender points. Increased pain with 4kg pressure at characteristic sites (suboccipital, trapezius, supraspinatus, gluteal, greater trochanter, knees, lateral epicondyle, low cervical, second rib).
physical_exam.Joints: Full range of motion. No synovitis. No joint swelling. No deformity.
physical_exam.Neurologic: Normal motor, sensory, reflexes. No focal deficits.

key_learning_points: Fibromyalgia = central pain processing disorder, NOT inflammatory arthritis. Affects 2-4% of population, F:M ratio 9:1
Diagnosis (ACR 2016): Widespread pain index ≥7 + Symptom severity scale ≥5 OR WPI 4-6 + SSS ≥9. Pain must be present ≥3 months. No longer requires tender point count
Associated: Chronic fatigue, non-restorative sleep, cognitive dysfunction ('fibro fog'), IBS, TMJ, anxiety/depression (50%), migraines
All labs are NORMAL - this is key to diagnosis and reassuring patients. Normal inflammatory markers differentiate from inflammatory arthritis

Treatment: Exercise (aerobic + strength training - GRADE A evidence), CBT, Amitriptyline 25-50mg at bedtime, Duloxetine or Milnacipran (SNRIs), Pregabalin or Gabapentin. Multidisciplinary approach. Avoid opioids (no benefit, high risk)
